articles.title: YOUR AI FEATURE IS ONLY AS GOOD AS THE DATA BEHIND IT.
articles.summary: Getting users' Notion docs, Jira tickets, and Google Drive files cleanly into your RAG pipeline across every tenant, with permissions intact, is the hard part no one talks about.

articles.title: SAVE YOUR SPOT →
articles.summary: (Can't attend? Make sure to sign up and get the recording afterwards.)

articles.title: ‘TOKENMAXXING' AS A WEIRD NEW TREND
articles.summary: "Tokenmaxxing" is a new trend where employees, motivated by internal leaderboards at companies like Meta, Microsoft, and Salesforce, maximize their AI token usage, resulting in financial waste, an emphasis on quantity over quality, and system outages. While Meta abolished its leaderboard after media backlash, Shopify successfully implemented a "usage dashboard" with human review and circuit breakers to encourage productive AI use and prevent wasteful burning.

articles.title: SHOPIFY SPEED OPTIMIZATION: FIXING THE REAL BOTTLENECKS
articles.summary: Structural bottlenecks slowing down Shopify stores can be addressed by optimizing above-the-fold content through reduced hero image sizes, placeholder videos, and efficient image compression. Managing JavaScript bloat via conditional loading and utilizing network prioritization techniques like preloading further improves the user experience further.

articles.title: HOW GOOD ENGINEERS WRITE BAD CODE AT BIG COMPANIES
articles.summary: Big tech companies often produce sloppy code because frequent internal reorganizations and high turnover mean systems are mostly maintained by engineers working outside their expertise. This technical debt is an intentional trade-off prioritized over deep expertise to maintain organizational flexibility and rapid redeployment capabilities.

articles.title: WHY I SPENT YEARS TRYING TO MAKE CSS STATES PREDICTABLE
articles.summary: Tasty is a compiler that addresses the unpredictability of traditional CSS by declaratively defining property states and priorities to generate mutually exclusive selectors. This approach removes cascade conflicts and guarantees deterministic behavior, making it easier to manage and extend complex design systems.

articles.title: DETAILS THAT MAKE INTERFACES FEEL BETTER
articles.summary: This is a collection of small UI polish details that compound into better-feeling interfaces: balanced text wrapping, concentric border radius (outer = inner + padding), tabular numbers, antialiased font smoothing, contextual icon animations, interruptible CSS transitions over keyframes, and more. The tips are also packaged as an open-source skill installable via npx.

articles.title: INTRODUCING GPT-5.5
articles.summary: GPT-5.5 is an advanced model capable of complex agentic coding and autonomous scientific research with improved token efficiency.

articles.title: AGENT VAULT
articles.summary: Agent Vault is an open-source HTTP credential proxy that secures AI agents by preventing them from directly handling sensitive API keys. By transparently injecting credentials at the network layer, it effectively eliminates risks like credential exfiltration and prompt injection vulnerabilities.

articles.title: AN UPDATE ON RECENT CLAUDE CODE QUALITY REPORTS
TORCHTPU: RUNNING PYTORCH NATIVELY ON TPUS AT GOOGLE SCALE
articles.summary: TorchTPU is a Google initiative that enables PyTorch models to run natively and efficiently on TPU hardware by prioritizing usability and high performance. The system supports complex distributed training. Plans for 2026 include public repository access and better support for dynamic shapes.

articles.title: CLONE THE REPO: BUILD A BETTER SELF-IMPROVING AGENT
articles.summary: Mitch used Inngest to keep his agent's eval pipeline running. Step-level insights were how he learned when it started to cheat [15].

articles.title: I AM BUILDING A CLOUD
articles.summary: Frustrated by the fundamental flaws and painful abstractions of existing cloud services, this dev is building a new cloud designed to better meet the needs of modern computing and the future demands of agent-driven software.

articles.title: GPT-5.5: MYTHOS-LIKE HACKING, OPEN TO ALL
articles.summary: GPT-5.5 delivers a "Mythos-like" and unprecedented leap in automated vulnerability detection and overall penetration testing capabilities, outperforming previous models and setting a new industry standard.

articles.title: YOUR HEX EDITOR SHOULD COLOR-CODE BYTES
articles.summary: Hex editors should color-code bytes, ideally with a granular scheme (like per nybble), because it uses human visual pattern recognition to enhance the ability to find and understand important structures and details within raw data.

articles.title: THE END OF RESPONSIVE IMAGES
articles.summary: The introduction of browser support for `sizes="auto"` and lazy loading finally automates and simplifies the once-complex management of responsive images for developers.
